# Lazy-loading catalogs

If your total catalog is under ~50KB gzipped, bundle every locale upfront and
skip this page. For larger catalogs, load only the active locale's data.

## How the generated module already splits per locale

The dev loop (or `npx autotranslate extract`) generates
`.translations/index.ts`. That module exports a `loadCatalog(locale)` function
backed by per-bucket dynamic `import()` calls:

```ts
// .translations/index.ts - GENERATED by autotranslate. Do not edit.
import type { Catalog, Locale } from '@autotranslate/core';

export const source = 'en' as const;
export const locales = ['en', 'de', 'fr'] as const;

const chunks: Record<
  string,
  ReadonlyArray<() => Promise<{ default: Catalog }>>
> = {
  en: [() => import('./en/0.json'), () => import('./en/3.json') /* ... */],
  de: [() => import('./de/0.json'), () => import('./de/3.json') /* ... */],
  fr: [() => import('./fr/0.json'), () => import('./fr/3.json') /* ... */],
};

export async function loadCatalog(locale: Locale): Promise<Catalog> {
  const parts = await Promise.all((chunks[locale] ?? []).map((load) => load()));
  return Object.assign({}, ...parts.map((m) => m.default));
}
```

Bundlers (Webpack, Turbopack, Vite) treat each `import()` as a separate chunk.
Only the requested locale's chunks are downloaded at runtime.

Import the module as a namespace - this is what `getT({ module })` and
`withRequestTranslator({ module })` expect:

```ts
import * as catalogModule from '../../.translations';
```

## Next.js - per-locale loading in layout

Call `loadCatalog(lang)` in the layout and pass the result to the provider:

```tsx
// app/[lang]/layout.tsx
import { TranslationProvider } from '@autotranslate/react';
import * as catalogModule from '../../.translations';

export default async function Layout({
  children,
  params,
}: {
  children: React.ReactNode;
  params: Promise<{ lang: string }>;
}) {
  const { lang } = await params;
  const [catalog, fallback] = await Promise.all([
    catalogModule.loadCatalog(lang),
    catalogModule.loadCatalog('en'),
  ]);

  return (
    <TranslationProvider locale={lang} catalog={catalog} fallback={fallback}>
      {children}
    </TranslationProvider>
  );
}
```

In server components, pass the module to `getT` and it calls `loadCatalog` for
you, memoized per (module, locale):

```ts
import { getT } from '@autotranslate/next';
import * as catalogModule from '../../.translations';

const t = await getT(lang, { module: catalogModule, fallback: 'en' });
```

Only the `lang` locale's chunks are fetched. The other locales are never
downloaded on this request.

## Vite - on-demand loading

By default the Vite plugin inlines every locale into `virtual:autotranslate`. To
keep the catalogs out of your entry chunk, import the virtual module
dynamically:

```ts
// src/lib/catalog.ts
import type { Catalog } from '@autotranslate/core';

let modulePromise: Promise<typeof import('virtual:autotranslate')> | null =
  null;

export function loadCatalog(locale: string): Promise<Catalog> {
  modulePromise ??= import('virtual:autotranslate');
  return modulePromise.then((m) => m.catalogs[locale] ?? {});
}
```

Vite produces one chunk for the dynamic import, so catalog data only ships when
first requested. For per-locale splitting, use the generated
`.translations/index.ts` module instead of the virtual module:

```ts
import * as catalogModule from '../.translations';

const catalog = await catalogModule.loadCatalog(locale);
```

Each locale's chunks become separate Vite chunks; the active locale is fetched
on first call. Trade-off: the generated module bypasses the virtual module's HMR
invalidation, so prefer it for production builds where catalog size matters, not
for dev.

## Custom source: Vercel KV / Edge Config

For runtimes where you store catalogs in KV instead of bundling them, use the
`load` callback in `getT`:

```ts
// app/api/translate/route.ts
import { getT } from '@autotranslate/next';
import { get } from '@vercel/edge-config';
import type { Catalog } from '@autotranslate/core';

export const runtime = 'edge';

export async function GET(request: Request) {
  const locale = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get('locale') ?? 'en';
  const translator = await getT(locale, {
    async load(l) {
      const blob = await get<Catalog>(`autotranslate:${l}`);
      return blob ?? {};
    },
  });
  return Response.json({ greeting: translator.t('Welcome') });
}
```

Wire your CI to push the contents of `.translations/<locale>/` into your KV
store on every catalog change.

## Cloudflare Workers KV

```ts
import { createTranslator } from '@autotranslate/core';
import type { Catalog } from '@autotranslate/core';

export default {
  async fetch(request: Request, env: { CATALOGS: KVNamespace }) {
    const locale = new URL(request.url).searchParams.get('locale') ?? 'en';
    const blob = await env.CATALOGS.get(`autotranslate:${locale}`, 'json');
    const catalog = (blob as Catalog | null) ?? {};

    const translator = createTranslator({ locale, catalog });
    return new Response(translator.t('Welcome'));
  },
};
```

## Tips

- **Cache the load promise, not the resolved value.** Concurrent calls for the
  same locale share one fetch, avoiding duplicate network requests.

- **Preload the next likely locale.** `<link rel="modulepreload">` on the
  most-likely-next-locale chunk avoids a flash on locale switch.

- **Don't lazy-load `fallback`.** The fallback catalog (usually English) should
  be available immediately so missed keys don't render blank.

- **Skip all of this when you can.** A 30KB catalog gzipped is smaller than one
  product photo. Don't reach for code-splitting until bundle size actually
  hurts.
